name: Be Mine Buttermilk Cake (2 of 2)
recipeInstructions:
1. For Truffle Cream: Stir chocolate cream and butter in a heavy medium saucepan over low heat until chocolate and butter meld and mixture is smooth. Mix in Chambord. Let stand at room temperature till very thick and spreadable, stirring occasionally, about 2 hours.
2. For Cake: Position rack in center of oven and preheat to 350 degrees F. Butter a 9-inch heart-shaped pan with 1-1/4 inch-high sides. Dust with flour; tap out excess.
3. Sift first 4 ingredients into a medium bowl.
4. Using an electric mixer at high speed, beat sugar and butter in a large bowl till fluffy. Add yolks 1 at a time, beating just to combine after each addition.
5. Using a rubber spatula, mix in dry ingredients alternately with buttermilk, beginning and ending with dry ingredients. Transfer batter to prepared pan.
6. Bake cake till tester inserted into center comes out clean, about 35 minutes. Turn out cake onto rack and cool.
7. Cut cake horizontally in half. Place bottom layer of cake on plate. Spread truffle cream over. Arrange top layer over and press gently to adhere.
8. Using a spatula, smooth cream on sides of cake if necessary. Chill until set, at least 1 hour.
9. For Glaze: Stir chocolate and butter in a heavy medium saucepan over low heat till melted and smooth. Remove from heat. Add corn syrup and liqueur and whisk till smooth. Let glaze stand till slightly thickened, stirring occasionally, about 30 minutes.
10. Place cake on rack set over baking sheet or large piece of foil. Pour glaze over cake, coating completely. Chill cake on rack till glaze is set, about 30 minutes.
11. Transfer cake to platter. (Can be made up to 1 day ahead. Cover with cake dome and chill. Bring to room temperature before serving.)
